name: Navalcarnero and Sevilla la Nueva Loop
name_original: Navalcarnero, Villamantilla, Villanueva de Perales, Sevilla la Nueva – ruta circular
description: This easy 21.0-mile (33.8 km) circular road cycling route near Navalcarnero offers a relaxed ride on well-paved surfaces.
region_intro: Road cycling around Navalcarnero offers diverse landscapes just southwest of Madrid, characterized by a rural setting with olive groves and vineyards. The area includes quiet secondary roads and charming villages, providing varied terrain for cyclists. The Guadarrama River is a prominent natural feature, with routes exploring its vicinity. The region's network of routes caters to different abilities, from gentle paths to more demanding ascents. Best road cycling routes around Navalcarnero The most popular road cycling route is Navalcarnero and Sevilla la Nueva Loop, an easy 21.0 miles (33.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete, offering a gentle ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is XXL Bicycle Sculpture – Navalcarnero Main Square loop from Navalcarnero, a moderate 46.6 miles (74.9 km) path. This route features an elevation gain of over 1800 feet, traversing rural areas and passing through the town center. Local road cyclists also love the XXL Bicycle Sculpture – Plaza de Segovia loop from Av. Arroyo Juncal - Av. Pinar, a 32.5 miles (52.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with an elevation gain of over 800 feet, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Navalcarnero is defined by rural landscapes, olive groves, and vineyards, with routes often following the Guadarrama River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate challenges. The routes in Navalcarnero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navalcarnero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Navalcarnero?
A: Navalcarnero offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 270 options to explore. These routes vary in distance and elevation, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Navalcarnero?
A: You'll experience a charming rural setting characterized by picturesque olive groves and vineyards. Many routes also follow the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4359319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama River&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse natural backdrops for your ride.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1600719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mortirolo Climb of Batres&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known local ascent. It features a two-kilometer climb with an average gradient of four percent, gaining approximately ninety meters in elevation.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: Absolutely. Navalcarnero offers several easy routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977178630/navalcarnero-and-sevilla-la-nueva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalcarnero and Sevilla la Nueva Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21-mile (33.8 km) trail that provides a gentle ride through the local landscape.

Q: Are there any unique cycling paths or greenways in the area?
A: Yes, a significant attraction for cyclists is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1394042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;. This converted narrow-gauge railway line is now exclusively for cyclists and pedestrians, offering a tranquil passage, including the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1394042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge over the Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: You can encounter several interesting spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1394042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Móstoles&lt;/a&gt; is a historic structure. For a break, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalcarnero Main Square&lt;/a&gt; offers a traditional Castilian setting with bars and restaurants. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2957869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Móstoles&lt;/a&gt;, a forest providing natural shade.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Navalcarnero?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;XXL Bicycle Sculpture – Navalcarnero Main Square loop from Navalcarnero&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 46.6-mile (74.9 km) circular path that traverses rural areas and the town center.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling routes in Navalcarnero?
A: The routes in Navalcarnero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the charming rural landscapes, and the network of quiet secondary roads.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling routes?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for those looking to cover more ground. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14926329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;XXL Bicycle Sculpture – Plaza de Segovia loop from La Dehesa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 81.2 km (50.5 miles) route, offering a substantial ride through the region.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or drink along the routes?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Segovia&lt;/a&gt; in Navalcarnero is an excellent spot for a break, offering numerous bars and restaurants with terraces. Many routes pass through or near charming villages where you can find local establishments for refreshments.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Navalcarnero?
A: The region is enjoyable year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer provides shade in areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2957869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Móstoles&lt;/a&gt;, while winter can offer crisp, clear rides, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly suitable for families?
A: For families, routes that utilize the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1394042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; are ideal. As a dedicated greenway, it offers a safe and traffic-free environment, perfect for a relaxed family ride.
tips: In Navalcarnero you can go from square to square, enjoying a well-deserved rest. There are squares with a long history, such as Plaza de Segovia, and recently created squares such as D. Francisco Sandoval, Plaza V Centenario, Plaza de la Virgen de la Cueva, etc.

In the hermitage of Nuestra Señora Virgen del Socorro there is a carving of the Virgin of the same name, in polychrome wood, whose antiquity is unknown. It is located at the exit of the municipality on the road that goes to Aldea del Fresno.

Villamantilla is a small municipality in the southwest of the Community of Madrid.
Road cyclists know it well, as it is in a quiet area and is highly recommended for practicing this type of cycling, although recent improvements to the guardrails have increased motorcycle traffic on these roads considerably.
His MTB march "La Rascaencinas" begins to make a name for himself in the Madrid circuit of popular marches.
https://bigjoe.es/ruta-mtb-ii-rascaencinas-de-villamantilla/

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Navalcarnero, Villamantilla, Villanueva de Perales, Sevilla la Nueva circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can conveniently start and park in any of the towns it passes through, including Navalcarnero, Villamantilla, Villanueva de Perales, or Sevilla la Nueva. Navalcarnero, being the largest, generally offers more parking options.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 28 minutes to complete, though your actual time may vary depending on your cycling pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is categorized as an 'Easy road ride' and is considered 'Great for any fitness level'. Its mostly well-paved surfaces make it very suitable for beginners looking for a relaxed outing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You can expect a smooth and enjoyable ride without significant technical challenges.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: While cycling this route, you'll pass through several charming towns and areas. Notable highlights include the Plaza de Segovia in Navalcarnero, the Roman Well, the Hermitage of Our Lady Virgin of Socorro, and the Villamantilla Town Center.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You'll find it briefly intersects with parts of the De Brunete a Navalcarnero por Sevilla la Nueva, Ruta Verde 5.7 Móstoles - Sevilla la Nueva, and the Ferrocarril abandonado Madrid Almorox, among others.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is on public roads and paved surfaces, it's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists on racebike routes due to safety concerns with traffic and the pace of cycling. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer and check local regulations for each town.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and is freely accessible. However, as it passes through the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama y su Entorno, always respect local regulations regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be quite hot in the Madrid region, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if cycling during that season.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like Navalcarnero, Villamantilla, Villanueva de Perales, and Sevilla la Nueva, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments and breaks.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when cycling within the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama y su Entorno?
faqs.answer: When cycling within the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama y su Entorno, it's important to stay on marked paths and roads, respect wildlife, and avoid littering. While no specific cycling permits are required for this route, always adhere to general park rules to preserve the natural environment.
